zotero.abstractNote: We consider the problem of finding sparse adversarial perturbations for image classifiers, which corresponds to the l1-constrained adversarial attack problem. While l1-attacks are important for evaluating the robustness of models against sparse noise, they are significantly harder to optimize than l0- or l-infinity-attacks. We propose a new attack, l1-APGD, which is based on the Auto-Projected Gradient Descent (APGD) framework. We show that l1-APGD is a strong and efficient attack that outperforms existing methods on a variety of datasets and models. We also provide a new, more robust evaluation protocol for l1-robustness.
